questions.questionText: All intrinsic muscles of the larynx are supplied by the recurrent laryngeal nerve EXCEPT:
questions.optionB: Cricothyroid (supplied by the external branch of superior laryngeal nerve — the only intrinsic laryngeal muscle with this innervation)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Cricothyroid = only intrinsic laryngeal muscle supplied by external branch of SLN, not RLN. Why correct: All intrinsic muscles → RLN (branch of vagus) EXCEPT cricothyroid → external branch of SLN. Cricothyroid: tenses vocal cords (pitch control). Internal branch of SLN: sensory above vocal cords. Why A wrong: Thyroarytenoid (vocalis) — RLN supply. Why C wrong: PCA — only ABductor of vocal cords, RLN supply (most important muscle). Why D wrong: LCA — adductor, RLN supply. Clinical relevance: RLN injury → vocal cord paralysis (unilateral: hoarseness; bilateral: stridor/airway emergency). SLN external branch injury → loss of high-pitched voice ('curtain sign' on laryngoscopy). Exam trap: PCA = only ABductor ('opens'); all others are ADductors ('close'). PCA palsy = cord in midline. Memory aid: 'Cricothyroid = CricoThyroid = Cannot be reached by RLN (uses SLN external).' Reference: Dhingra – Larynx Anatomy.
questions.conceptTag: Cricothyroid SLN external RLN intrinsic larynx
